full_text:
Heatwave Alert: Schools, Colleges closed in Deogarh till April 23
Deogarh district administration has ordered closure of all educational institutions, from April 20 to April 23 due to severe heatwave conditions
Deogarh: The Deogarh district administration has ordered the closure of all educational institutions, including Anganwadi Centres, schools, colleges and technical institutions, from April 20 to April 23 due to severe heatwave conditions.
According to the official notification, the closure will remain in effect until further orders. However, examinations scheduled during this period will continue as planned. Authorities have advised that exams may be conducted during morning hours to avoid peak heat conditions.
The administration has also directed heads of institutions to ensure adequate drinking water facilities and implement necessary heatwave safety measures for students and staff attending examinations.

title: Heatwave : Schools, Colleges Shut in Deogarh District Till April 23 - Sambad English
search_term: heatwave (Angul OR Balangir OR Balasore OR Bargarh OR Bhadrak OR Boudh OR Cuttack OR Deogarh OR Dhenkanal OR Gajapati OR Ganjam OR Jagatsinghapur OR Jajpur OR Jharsuguda OR Kalahandi OR Kandhamal OR Kendrapara OR "Kendujhar (Keonjhar)" OR Khordha OR Koraput OR Malkangiri OR Mayurbhanj OR Nabarangpur OR Nayagarh OR Nuapada OR Puri OR Rayagada OR Sambalpur OR Sonepur OR Sundargarh) Odisha
full_text:
Deogarh: The Deogarh district administration has ordered the closure of all educational institutions, including Anganwadi Centres, schools, colleges and technical institutions, from April 20 to April 23 due to severe heatwave conditions.
The directive was issued by the Collector following a Red Warning for the district by the India Meteorological Department (IMD), Bhubaneswar. The order aims to protect students from extreme heat as temperatures continue to rise across the region.
According to the official notification, the closure will remain in effect until further orders. However, examinations scheduled during this period will continue as planned. Authorities have advised that exams may be conducted during morning hours to avoid peak heat conditions.
The administration has also directed heads of institutions to ensure adequate drinking water facilities and implement necessary heatwave safety measures for students and staff attending examinations.
